title: After-Action Report — WAR/OAA fix: position-neutral OAA + reliability-regressed Field Runs into WAR
scope: Scope: o27v2/analytics/expanded.py (OAA/Field Runs), o27v2/web/app.py (WAR defensive input + baselines), o27v2/web/templates/o27i_player.html, o27v2/web/templates/o27i_advanced.html, tests/test_stat_invariants.py.

rationale.item: The season card's WAR read a rating-derived scout DRS while the Savant page showed event-based OAA/Field Runs — two independent defensive pipelines that never reconciled (a Memphis Koalas CF showed WAR 3.65 with a +0.1 scout-defense term next to a −19.9 Field Runs on the other surface).

The positional artifact was real: infielders systematically positive, outfielders systematically negative — OF regulars absorb every extra-base hit in their zone while being measured against an EV/LA out-rate inflated by easy infield outs in the same bin.

OAA barely tracks skill: correlation of de-biased OAA to the player's actual defense rating ≈ 0.10; defense enters only as a team-level range shift plus sparse individual gems. Feeding raw OAA into WAR would inject ±1.8 wins of fielding noise per player.

actions.description: build_fielding_value made two-pass: out_rate[zone, EV, LA] baseline (with (EV, LA) fallback) over all non-HR BIP; output gains reliability; frv is the regressed run value (oaa × 0.78 × reliability); leaderboards sort by frv
_league_baselines_compute publishes fielding_runs and fielding_chances maps so every WAR consumer sees the displayed Field Runs without touching ~18 _aggregate_batter_rows call sites
WAR block: def_runs is the regressed Field Runs for fielders with ≥ _WAR_FIELDING_MIN_CHANCES (25) chances, else scout DRS projection; def_runs_source records which; dwar = def_runs / rpw
Tooltips updated to explain position-neutrality, HR exclusion, the reliability regression, and that FRV is the WAR-bound figure
Invariant 11 added: WAR def_runs == displayed FRV (source must be field) for qualified fielders, plus WAR == war_off + dwar

risk_posture: Scout DRS is retained as its own column and used only as the small-sample fallback.
Attribution unchanged (exact engine fielder on outs; trajectory-zone → regular on hits).
K is a documented, tunable constant (`_FIELDING_REGRESSION_K`).
Did not touch ratings or the sim engine. … out of scope and deliberately left alone.

validation_claims.claim: After conditioning expected-out on zone + dropping HRs: every position centers at ~0 (CF −2.4, SS −0.5, LF −3.4, …), stdev drops to 23 (from 31)
validation_claims.check_method: Rebuild a 12-team 396-game sim DB and recompute OAA by position

validation_claims.claim: WAR def_runs equals displayed frv to <0.05 runs for every qualified fielder (invariant 11, plus a direct end-to-end check on real route rows)
validation_claims.check_method: Run tests/test_stat_invariants.py against a seeded DB

validation_claims.claim: o27/tests o27v2/tests → 235 passed, 1 skipped; tests/test_stat_invariants.py → 12 passed (was 11)
validation_claims.check_method: pytest o27/tests o27v2/tests and the invariant suite
Request the routes via test client

validation_claims.claim: Verified on a fresh initdb 12teams + 396-game sim (O27V2_DB_PATH=/tmp/recon.db)
Regression K=400 narrows FRV p10/p90 from −26.4/+23.2 to −7.6/+3.9, dWAR span from −1.84…+1.86 to −0.75…+0.39
validation_claims.check_method: Recompute FRV distribution on the sim DB with and without regression

non_actions.item: Did not touch ratings or the sim engine (per-fielder catch influence in resolve_contact)
non_actions.reason: The weak individual-fielder signal is an engine property (defense is largely team-level) … out of scope and deliberately left alone

non_actions.item: Rating→OAA sample-growth continuous blend (vs hard switch at 25 chances)
non_actions.reason: is the natural next step and would smooth the boundary

non_actions.item: Did not add baserunning (BSR) to WAR
non_actions.reason: Still surfaced-but-not-summed; a separate mitigation. … BSR is the remaining gap

residual_risks: K = 400 is calibrated, not derived — set from one 396-game DB to land a believable fielding band.
Display nuance: sub-25-chance fielders still show an OAA/FRV on their o27i page while their WAR uses the scout projection; the invariant scopes to qualified fielders accordingly.
open_hooks: _FIELDING_REGRESSION_K exposed for tuning like the engine's RES_* knobs.
Continuous rating/OAA blend as chances grow.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — WAR/OAA fix: position-neutral OAA + reliability-regressed Field Runs into WAR
Why — the verification that drove the design
What I did NOT do / follow-ups
unmapped_content: Before-fix positional mean OAA table: 1B +2.2, 2B +3.3, 3B +5.5, SS +11.5, LF −17.5, CF −16.6, RF −9.2; spread stdev 31, range −72.6…+49.9; the Koala's −25.5 was mild. Engine constants: DEFENSE_RANGE_SHIFT_SCALE = 0.15 (max ~10% single↔out flip), GEM_BASE_* ≈ 0.05–0.075; post-inversion corr EV→hit 0.364. reliability = chances / (chances + K), K = 400; full-season qualified fielder lands at 0.2–0.46 weight. The Koala reconciled: OAA −25.5 at ~250 chances → reliability ≈ 0.39 → regressed ≈ −7.7 Field Runs → ≈ −0.4…−0.5 dWAR; WAR 3.65 → ~3.1. Branch claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG. Predecessors: docs/aar-war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas.md (the finding), docs/aar-stat-surface-reconciliation-comprehensive.md (the deep-dive); this AAR is the fix.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date:' header (2026-06-06). No explicit delegation/owner-decision sentences; decisions are agent-side design choices recorded under risk_posture/non_actions.
